The Nut Job 2: Nutty by Nature (2017)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $28,370,522Details
International Box Office $43,659,136
Worldwide Box Office $72,029,658
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $1,983,393
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,972,936
Total $3,956,329
Further financial details...

Synopsis

A comedy in fictional Oakton that follows the travails of Surly, a mischievous squirrel, and his rat friend Buddy, who plan a nut store heist of outrageous proportions and unwittingly find themselves embroiled in a much more complicated and hilarious adventure.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$8,342,311 (29.4% of total gross)
Legs:3.40 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:39.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$40,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,003 opening theaters/4,003 max. theaters, 4.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $37,827,363

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 11th, 2017 (Wide) by Open Road
International Releases: August 11th, 2017 (Wide), released as The Nut Job 2: Nutty By Nature (United Kingdom)
August 17th, 2017 (Wide), released as Locos por las Nueces 2 (Mexico)
August 18th, 2017 (Special Engagement) (Slovakia)
August 18th, 2017 (Wide) (Poland)
August 25th, 2017 (Wide), released as Velká oříšková loupež 2 (Czech Republic)
Video Release: October 31st, 2017 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for action and some rude humor.
(Rating bulletin 2477 (Cert #50832), 5/24/2017)
Running Time: 91 minutes
Franchise: The Nut Job
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animal Lead
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Shot in 3-D
Plot point: Food, Government Corruption, Land Developer, Mayor, Talking Animals
Social setting: Amusement Park
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Digital Animation
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Toonbox Entertainment, Red Rover Films, Gulfstream Pictures
Production Countries: United States, Republic of Korea, Canada
Languages: English
